calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: MECH-057b Completion Gate v2 — adds update_after_execution so running_variance evolves
items.description: Rewrite of EXQ-048b. Root cause: update_after_execution() never called so _running_variance frozen at 0.5 > commit_threshold=0.40 forever. Fix: call agent.e3.update_after_execution(z_world_t, harm_prev<0) at each step start. PASS: C1 committed_step_count>=10, C2 hold_rate_during_committed>0.5, C3 propagation_count>0, C4 cal_gap_approach>0.
items.failure_reason: C2 FAIL: hold_rate_during_committed=0.000 <= 0.5 | harm_scale=args.harm_scale, | 3. **EXQ-048c**: Adds `agent.e3.update_after_execution(z_world_t, harm_{t-1})` at

items.title: MECH-090 BetaGate v2 — adds update_after_execution so running_variance evolves
items.description: Rewrite of EXQ-049b. Same root cause: _running_variance frozen at 0.5 > commit_threshold=0.40, gate never exercises. Fix: agent.e3.update_after_execution(z_world_t, harm_prev<0) each step. PASS: C1 committed_hold_concordance>0.6, C2 uncommitted_release_concordance>0.5, C3 hold_count>0, C4 propagation_count>0.
items.failure_reason: C3 FAIL: hold_count=0 == 0 | harm_scale=args.harm_scale, | 3. **EXQ-049c**: `update_after_execution(z_world_t, harm_{t-1})` called each step

items.title: MECH-090 BetaGate v3 — chicken-and-egg fix (direct update_running_variance)
items.description: Fixes the deadlock that killed 049b and 049c: post_action_update() guards variance update behind _committed_trajectory, which is never set because variance never drops — circular. Fix 1: call agent.e3.update_running_variance(wf_err) directly after world_forward loss (no guard). Fix 2: is_committed = _running_variance < commit_threshold (not _committed_trajectory probe, which is reset by post_action_update every step). PASS: C1 committed_hold_concordance>0.6, C2 uncommitted_release_concordance>0.5, C3 hold_count>0, C4 propagation_count>0, C5 no fatal errors.

items.title: ARC-007 Path Memory Ablation (Proper) — residue field zeroing degrades hippocampal proposals
items.description: Prerequisite: EXQ-042 PASS. Tests ARC-007 STRICT (Q-020): hippocampal proposals are value-flat, relying entirely on residue field geometry. Protocol: P1 train (600 eps), P2 eval intact, P3 ablate (zero residue field via monkeypatch), P4 eval ablated, P5 restore, P6 eval restored. PASS: C1 hippo_quality_gap_intact>0, C2 ablated<=intact*0.5, C3 restored>=ablated, C4 cal_gap_approach>0.03, C5 no errors.

items.title: SD-010 Reafference Isolation — z_harm survives reafference correction (EXQ-027b retest)
items.description: Direct retest of EXQ-027b with SD-010. z_harm from HarmEncoder(harm_obs) is architecturally separate from z_world and never enters the reafference pipeline. Tests: calibration_gap_z_harm>0.03 (same threshold as EXQ-027b), harm_pred_std_z_harm>0.01, reafference_r2_z_world>0.10, n_agent_hazard_steps>=5, identity_test_pass==1.0.

items.title: MECH-100 Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Encoder Loss (hazard_harm=0.02, alpha=0.9)
items.description: FIXED: hazard_harm=0.02, alpha_world=0.9, warmup=1000. Tests SD-009/MECH-100: CE event classifier (Linear(world_dim,3)) backprops through z_world encoder. Labels: 0=none, 1=env_caused_hazard, 2=agent_caused_hazard. λ_event=0.5. PASS C1: calibration_gap>0.05, C2: event_classification_acc>0.5, C3: selectivity_margin>0.005.

items.title: Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Encoder Loss — Seed 1
items.description: Repeat of EXQ-020 with seed=1 for robustness.

items.title: Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Loss — Strong λ=2.0 (hazard_harm=0.02, alpha=0.9)
items.description: FIXED: hazard_harm=0.02, alpha_world=0.9, warmup=1000. EXQ-020 variant with λ_event=2.0 (4× stronger contrastive signal). If EXQ-020 achieves classification accuracy but not calibration_gap, tests whether stronger CE loss helps. Risk: forgetting of E1/E2 reconstruction.

items.title: MECH-090 BetaGate v4 — two-condition design (trained vs fresh agent)
items.description: Fixes single-agent limitation of EXQ-049d: trained agent always committed (variance~0), fresh agent always uncommitted (variance=precision_init=0.5>threshold). C1/C3 from trained agent (gate elevates), C2/C4 from fresh agent (gate releases). No artificial variance manipulation needed — both conditions at stable extremes.

items.title: MECH-104 Volatility Interrupt — jitter approximation enables de-commitment on surprise harm
items.description: Tests MECH-104 (LC-NE volatility interrupt) V3 approximation. Two conditions: baseline (no spike, confirms variance collapse) vs surprise-spike (adds spike_magnitude=0.05 to running_variance on harm contact). PASS: C1 baseline n_uncommitted=0, C2 spike n_uncommitted>100, C3 variance rises after harm contact, C4 calibration_gap preserved, C5 no fatal errors.

items.title: Q-007 z_beta Volatility Injection — NE/LC analog via running_variance input
items.description: Fix for Q-007 architectural gap (EXQ-051 FAIL). Injects E3._running_variance as scalar into beta_encoder via LatentStackConfig.volatility_signal_dim=1. Two conditions: stable (fixed hazard positions) vs volatile (randomised each episode), same mean hazard count. Yu & Dayan 2005 (PMID 15944135): NE encodes unexpected uncertainty = running_variance spike. With injection: volatile agent should have higher z_beta norm (C1), Pearson r(rv,z_beta)>0.3 (C2), and z_beta rises within 20 eps after stable→volatile transfer (C3).
